description_long: This object is a round, slightly irregular citrus fruit with a textured, dimpled skin. Its surface displays a mix of yellow and green hues, with some subtle brownish spots and a small, slightly indented stem area at the top. The skin appears thick and somewhat rough, typical of an orange that is not fully ripe. The overall shape is nearly spherical but with minor flattening at the poles. The fruit's coloration and surface imperfections suggest it may have been picked from an outdoor tree, possibly in an urban park or garden. The object is solid and compact, with a dense, juicy interior implied by its form and typical of oranges.
description: A round, yellow-green citrus fruit with a dimpled, rough skin and a small stem indentation. The surface shows natural imperfections and color variation.
description_view_0: This view highlights the top of the orange with a visible stem indentation and a gradient from yellow to green.
description_view_1: This angle shows more of the yellow-green coloration and some brownish speckling, with the stem area less pronounced.
description_view_2: This side presents a mostly uniform yellow-green surface with subtle dimpling and minimal visible blemishes.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the roundness and the smoother, more yellow side of the orange, with less visible stem detail.
attribute_location_or_placement: sidewalk, park ground, street market, or outdoor urban area
